    Sign in

    Your move to a Surface Copilot+ PC, made simple

    Follow 3 easy steps to transfer your important files and settings to a powerful new Surface with Windows 11.

    • Upgrade with confidence
    • Explore Surface devices
    • Get to know Windows 11
    • Support & FAQs

    It’s as easy as…

    Get your Windows PC ready

    Check if your old PC is running the latest version of Windows.

    Learn how

    Move your files

    Back up your files to OneDrive to make them available to you on your brand new or next PC.1

    Learn how

    Sign in to Windows 11

    When you start your new Surface, sign in with the same Microsoft account and Windows Backup2 will help move your files, apps, and settings over.

    Learn how

    Which version of Windows am I running?

    To find your Windows version and edition, you can use one of the following methods:

    • Right-click on Start and select Settings.
    • In the Settings app on your Windows device, select System > About.
    • Use the following link to quickly open settings:
    Open About Settings

    Under Windows specifications, check which edition and version of Windows your device is running and run recommended updates.

    Back up your files

    A simple way to back up your files to your OneDrive is to use Windows Backup. Windows Backup is an easy, single stop for all your backup needs. Your Microsoft account comes with 5 GB of OneDrive cloud storage and you can upgrade to a qualifying Microsoft 365 subscription, which offers additional storage and premium features. See available options. Windows Backup will back up the folders you specify to your OneDrive and they can be transferred when you move to a new Surface Copilot+ PC. Windows Backup will also restore your app list and selected settings* **.* Backs up files, settings, and apps from PCs running Windows 10 or 11. Requires Microsoft account; data transfers exceeding 5 GB require Microsoft subscription or trial. Some apps require manual reinstallation from the original app provider and some may not be compatible with Arm devices. See WOA for Arm-compatible apps and Windows Backup for details.** When you open apps for the first time on your new Windows 11 PC, some apps will reinstall when you first open them. Other apps may require you to reinstall them manually from the original app provider.

    To open Windows Backup, search for it in the Start menu:

    1. Select Start.
    2. Type backup and select Windows Backup from the results.
    3. Select Manage backup.
    4. Expand Folders and select which of your user folders you want to back up to OneDrive. You can back up your Desktop, Documents, Pictures, Videos, and Music folders by toggling the switch for each folder to On.
    All set!

    Learn more

    Sign in to your new Surface with a Microsoft account

    When you power on your Surface for the first time* **, you’ll be guided through a few quick steps to get set up and ready to go.* When you open apps for the first time on your new Windows 11 PC, some apps will reinstall when you first open them. Other apps may require you to reinstall them manually from the original app provider.** Windows 11 Home and Pro editions require internet connectivity and a Microsoft account to complete device setup on first use.

    1. Sign in with the Microsoft account you used to back up your old PC.
    2. Follow the prompts to make your new Surface feel like home.

    Learn more

    Here’s what moves with you—and what doesn’t

    Save what you love and Windows Backup will transfer it to your new Surface computer.1

    Transfers

    • Your files, documents, and photos
    • Selected Windows settings and preferences1
    • App list (not the apps themselves)1

    Doesn't transfer

    • Some apps and device‑specific settings—you may need to reinstall apps from Microsoft Store or the app’s provider and some may not be compatible with Arm devices1

    Managed/school devices

    • Your organization’s policies may change the experience

    Explore Surface PCs

    It’s easy to move to Windows 11 on your new Surface Copilot+ PC.

    Find the best Surface for you

    Whether you need a Surface Laptop or Surface Pro for your business or everyday life, find the right fit with brilliant AI features, security features built in, and a choice of screen sizes.

    Shop Surface Copilot+ PCs Shop Surface for Business
    Limited-time offer

    Get up to $800 cash back with trade-in

    Buy a select new Surface Copilot+ PC and get extra cash back with a qualifying trade-in3. More devices now eligible.*

    * Terms and eligibility requirements apply. Available trade in-offers vary by country and over time.

    Learn more

    Save on Surface Copilot+ PCs

    Find current deals on Surface Laptop and Surface Pro devices at Microsoft Store.

    Shop deals

    Get to know Windows 11

    From AI-powered features to built-in security protections, it’s the Windows you know, and more.

    The power of AI

    Advanced AI tools and apps like Microsoft Copilot help you get things done more easily and effectively.

    Learn more

    A new era of security

    Windows 11 brings layers and layers of security measures to help keep your digital life in safe hands—yours.

    Learn more

    Frequently asked questions

    |

    • Yes. A Microsoft account is required to set up a device with Windows 11 Home or Windows 11 Pro. Your Microsoft account comes with 5 GB OneDrive cloud storage and access to free web and mobile versions of Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. If you don’t have a Microsoft account, it’s easy to sign up using your existing or new email address here.

    • OneDrive is a Microsoft cloud storage service that lets you store your personal files in one place, share them with others, and access them from any device connected to the internet. Learn more about OneDrive.

    • Learn how to add accounts in OneDrive.

    • A Microsoft account includes 5 GB of cloud storage with OneDrive. If you need more space for your files, you can upgrade to a qualifying Microsoft 365 subscription, which offers additional storage and premium features. See available options.

    • Yes, you can bring your files with you using an external storage device such as a USB drive, SD card, or external hard drive. Learn how.

    • Yes. You can install or activate compatible versions of Office on your new computer using your product key.

      Activate Office on a new PC >

      How to install Office >

    • Windows Backup helps you move files, settings, and a list of your apps when setting up a new Windows 11 PC. It works with PCs running Windows 10 or later and restores on Windows 11, version 22H2 or higher.

      Keep in mind that apps themselves aren’t automatically transferred or installed. When you open an app for the first time on your new PC, some can be reinstalled directly from the Microsoft Store. Others may need to be reinstalled manually from the original provider. Geographic restrictions may apply. See WOA for Arm-compatible apps and Windows Backup for details.

    • Microsoft Store offers a Trade-in Program powered by Teladvance in select countries. You may be eligible for cash back if your device meets the criteria for offers currently available in your country. Just answer a few questions about your device’s brand, model, and condition to see if it qualifies. Get started by checking your device’s estimated trade-in value. Available trade-in offers and eligibility requirements apply and may vary over time.

    • Microsoft announced last October the Extended Security Updates (ESU) consumer program that gives the option to receive critical and important security updates for eligible Windows 10 PCs enrolled in the program through October 13, 2026. The program provides you with more time to stay protected on your current Windows 10 device before moving to a new Surface PC.

      For additional information on Consumer ESU options, please visit ESU Settings or the ESU support page.

      For additional information on Commercial ESU program, please visit the Microsoft Learn page.

    • Screens simulated. Features and app availability may vary by region.
    • [1] Backs up files, settings, and apps from PCs running Windows 10 or 11. Requires Microsoft account; data transfers exceeding 5 GB require Microsoft subscription or trial. Some apps require manual reinstallation from the original app provider and some may not be compatible with Arm devices. See WOA for Arm-compatible apps and Windows Backup for details.
    • [2] Using Windows Backup to restore to a Windows 11 PC requires you to sign in during initial device setup with the same Microsoft account you used for Windows 10 PC backup.
    • [3] Available through the Microsoft Store in select countries only. See site for details.
